title: spatalla incurva
intro: Spatalla incurva, also known as the Hooked Spatalla, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Spatalla incurva include its ability to relieve coughs and congestion, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, with the leaves and flowers being used to make a herbal remedy.
